title: Matayba scrobiculata
intro: Matayba scrobiculata, also known as the "Andiroba" tree, is a native plant species from the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. This plant has been widely used in traditional medicine, boasting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Matayba scrobiculata include fatty acids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Matayba scrobiculata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as oil, cream, and capsules, which are often used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Matayba scrobiculata is classified as a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae, commonly known as the soapberry or soapbark family. The Sapindaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that includes over 1,500 species, spread across 130 genera. This family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Matayba scrobiculata is specifically found in the Americas, where it is considered to be a valuable plant for its medicinal and timber uses. It is a deciduous tree that grows up to 20 meters tall, with a broad, rounded crown and dark green leaves.

The plant is believed to have antimicrobial effects, which could be beneficial in preventing infections and promoting wound healing. parts: Matayba scrobiculata, a plant native to Central and South America, is valued for its medicinal properties.
